name: Banker
summary: A financier who accepts deposits, extends credit, and manages payment systems—the institutional backbone of commercial exchange from medieval benches to global capital markets.
duties: Evaluate loan risk, manage accounts, comply with regulation, advise clients on treasury, and oversee branch or trading operations.
skills: Credit analysis, relationship management, regulatory literacy, liquidity planning, fraud detection.
tools: Ledger systems, vaults, wire networks, core banking software, risk models.
regions: Italian city-states, London, New York, Hong Kong, and every modern banking hub.

events.label: Bank of England charter
events.description: Central banking models spread, anchoring national credit and note issuance.

educationPath: Finance degree, banking exams, compliance training, graduate programs.
modernSectors: Retail banking, corporate banking, central banks, development finance.

name: Stock Trader
summary: A market operator who buys and sells equity securities for profit—evolving from open-outcry exchange floors to algorithmic desks that price companies in milliseconds.
duties: Analyze issuers, execute orders, manage positions, monitor news flow, and report P&L.
skills: Market timing, risk control, pattern recognition, emotional discipline, compliance awareness.
tools: Trading terminals, order books, Bloomberg screens, co-location servers.
regions: Amsterdam, London, New York, Tokyo, Shanghai—global listed markets.
originLabel: Dutch East India Company share trading

events.label: NASDAQ electronic market
events.description: Screen-based trading accelerates volume and democratizes access to equities.

educationPath: Finance or economics degree, Series licenses, prop-desk apprenticeship.
modernSectors: Investment banks, hedge funds, proprietary trading, retail brokerages.

name: Insurance Agent
summary: A seller of risk-transfer contracts who helps households and firms hedge fire, liability, health, and life contingencies—rooted in marine underwriters and Lloyd's coffee-house deals.
duties: Assess client needs, quote premiums, bind policies, handle renewals, and process claims referrals.
skills: Needs analysis, persuasion, product knowledge, underwriting basics, ethics.
tools: Policy forms, actuarial tables, CRM systems, comparative raters.
regions: London marine market, U.S. mutuals, European bancassurance, Asian life giants.

events.label: Franklin fire mutual
events.description: Benjamin Franklin's Philadelphia contribution fund popularizes property insurance in America.

educationPath: Insurance licensing exams, continuing education, carrier product schools.
modernSectors: Life, P&C, health, commercial lines, independent and captive agencies.

name: Real Estate Agent
summary: A licensed intermediary who markets land and buildings, negotiates sales and leases, and guides clients through title and financing—professionalized as urban property markets exploded in the 19th century.
duties: List properties, host showings, draft offers, coordinate inspections, and close transactions.
skills: Negotiation, local market knowledge, marketing, contract literacy, client counseling.
tools: MLS databases, lockboxes, drones for photography, e-signature platforms.
regions: Americas, Europe, Middle East, Asia-Pacific property markets.
originLabel: Urban brokerage and land speculation booms

events.label: REALTOR trademark
events.description: National Association of Real Estate Boards codifies professional standards in the U.S.

educationPath: Pre-licensing courses, state exams, broker sponsorship, CE credits.
modernSectors: Residential, commercial, industrial, vacation rentals, property management overlap.

name: Venture Capitalist
summary: An equity investor who funds early-stage companies in exchange for ownership and governance rights—crystallized in Silicon Valley after World War II as technology commercialization accelerated.
duties: Source deals, conduct due diligence, negotiate term sheets, join boards, and manage portfolio exits.
skills: Pattern recognition, founder coaching, valuation, sector thesis building, network leverage.
tools: Cap tables, data rooms, term-sheet templates, CRM, portfolio dashboards.
regions: Silicon Valley, Boston, Tel Aviv, London, Bangalore, Shenzhen.
originLabel: American Research and Development Corporation era

events.label: Dot-com venture wave
events.description: Internet startups attract institutional VC scale and global copycat funds.

educationPath: MBA, operator experience, angel investing, VC associate programs.
modernSectors: Seed funds, growth equity, corporate venture, government innovation programs.

name: Auditor
summary: An independent examiner who tests financial records for accuracy and compliance—descended from medieval account scrutiny and now governed by statutory audit regimes after industrial scandals.
duties: Plan audits, test controls, sample transactions, issue opinions, and report fraud risks.
skills: Accounting standards, skepticism, sampling, documentation, stakeholder communication.
tools: Working papers, audit software, confirmations, data analytics scripts.
regions: Big Four networks, national firms, government audit offices worldwide.

events.label: Sarbanes-Oxley Act
events.description: U.S. reforms tighten auditor independence and internal-control attestation after Enron.

educationPath: Accounting degree, CPA or chartered accountant exams, firm training.
modernSectors: Public company audit, internal audit, government, nonprofit assurance.

name: Marketing Manager
summary: A commercial strategist who positions products, plans campaigns, and measures demand—emerging with mass production and advertising agencies as firms competed for national consumer attention.
duties: Define segments, set budgets, brief creative teams, launch channels, and report ROI.
skills: Brand strategy, analytics, copy judgment, vendor management, consumer psychology.
tools: CRM, ad platforms, survey tools, marketing automation, attribution models.
regions: U.S. consumer goods pioneers, global FMCG, digital-native brands.

events.label: Digital marketing era
events.description: Search and display ads shift spend from print and broadcast to measurable online funnels.

educationPath: Marketing or business degree, agency rotations, analytics certifications.
modernSectors: CPG, tech, retail, B2B SaaS, entertainment, nonprofit fundraising.

name: Investment Banker
summary: A corporate finance specialist who underwrites securities, advises mergers, and structures deals—institutionalized when railroads and industrials needed huge public capital raises.
duties: Build pitch books, model valuations, run due diligence, syndicate offerings, and negotiate covenants.
skills: Financial modeling, presentation, stamina, regulatory navigation, client politics.
tools: Excel, PowerPoint, data rooms, deal tombstones, league tables.
regions: Wall Street, City of London, Frankfurt, Hong Kong, Dubai IFC.

events.label: Glass-Steagall separation
events.description: U.S. law splits commercial and investment banking until late-20th-century repeal debates.

educationPath: Top-tier finance MBA, analyst programs, licensing exams.
modernSectors: M&A advisory, equity and debt capital markets, restructuring, FIG.

name: Commodity Trader
summary: A dealer in standardized goods—grain, metals, oil, cotton—who hedges producers and speculates on supply shocks, with roots in warehouse receipts and Chicago pit trading.
duties: Monitor supply chains, execute futures and forwards, manage basis risk, and settle deliveries.
skills: Macro awareness, logistics knowledge, contract law, position sizing, weather and geopolitics reading.
tools: Futures terminals, warehouse receipts, charter-party data, satellite crop imagery.
regions: Chicago, Rotterdam, Singapore, Geneva trading houses.
originLabel: Chicago Board of Trade grain contracts

events.label: Oil futures launch
events.description: Energy contracts globalize commodity hedging beyond agricultural pits.

educationPath: Economics, commodities house training, FCM licensing.
modernSectors: Agriculture, energy, metals, soft commodities, merchant trading firms.

name: Stockbroker
summary: A registered representative who executes securities orders for clients and may advise on portfolios—once a floor clerk shouting bids, now often a remote advisor behind a screen.
duties: Take client orders, recommend allocations, maintain suitability records, and settle trades.
skills: Product knowledge, compliance, relationship building, market color, documentation.
tools: Brokerage platforms, order tickets, client statements, KYC systems.
regions: New York, London, Mumbai, São Paulo retail and institutional channels.

events.label: U.S. commission deregulation
events.description: Negotiated rates end fixed commissions, reshaping retail brokerage economics.

educationPath: Series 7 and state exams, firm training, CFA optional.
modernSectors: Full-service wirehouses, discount brokers, RIAs, bank brokerage.

name: Actuary
summary: A quantitative specialist who prices insurance and pension risk using mortality and event statistics—one of the oldest formal "data science" professions, tied to life tables since the 17th century.
duties: Build models, set reserves, certify solvency, advise on product design, and testify on liabilities.
skills: Probability, programming, regulation, communication of uncertainty, business judgment.
tools: Actuarial software, mortality tables, stochastic simulators, Excel and R.
regions: British life offices, North American insurers, global consulting firms.
originLabel: Life annuity and mortality table science

events.label: Society of Actuaries growth
events.description: Professional exams standardize credentialing across life, health, and pension practice.

educationPath: Math or actuarial science degree, SOA or IFoA exam series.
modernSectors: Life and health insurance, pensions, enterprise risk, government programs.

name: Mortgage Broker
summary: An intermediary who matches borrowers with lenders and packages home-loan applications—surged with securitized mortgage markets and online rate shopping in the late 20th century.
duties: Collect financial documents, shop lender programs, explain terms, and shepherd closings.
skills: Credit guidelines, disclosure rules, client education, pipeline management.
tools: Loan origination systems, credit pulls, automated underwriting engines.
regions: United States, UK, Australia, Canada residential markets.

events.label: Subprime crisis scrutiny
events.description: Broker compensation and underwriting standards face intense regulatory reform.

educationPath: NMLS licensing, lender overlays training, compliance CE.
modernSectors: Residential purchase and refinance, non-QM niches, correspondent lending.

name: Pawnbroker
summary: A collateral lender who advances cash against personal goods and sells unredeemed pledges—an ancient bridge between money-changing and consumer credit for households without bank access.
duties: Appraise items, write pawn tickets, store collateral, collect interest, and retail forfeits.
skills: Valuation of jewelry and electronics, loss prevention, regulatory compliance, haggling.
tools: Scales, testers, safes, ticketing systems, retail display cases.
regions: Europe, Americas, Southeast Asia—ubiquitous in dense urban districts.

events.label: British Pawnbrokers Act
events.description: Statutory frameworks balance consumer protection with licensed lending.

educationPath: State pawnbroker licenses, appraisal courses, AML training.
modernSectors: High-street pawn, online collateral lending, buy-sell-trade overlap.

name: Money Changer
summary: A street or booth operator who converts currencies and tests coin authenticity—essential before widespread banks and still visible at borders, bazaars, and tourist districts.
duties: Quote exchange rates, count and verify notes, manage float, and hedge overnight positions.
skills: Mental arithmetic, counterfeit detection, multilingual service, rate monitoring.
tools: Scales, counterfeit pens, UV lamps, rate boards, cash drawers.
regions: Medieval fairs, Ottoman sarrafs, modern airport kiosks and forex shops.

events.label: Euro cash introduction
events.description: Single currency reduces intra-European changer demand while global travel sustains booths.

name: Merchant
summary: A trader who buys goods wholesale and resells for margin—historically the class that financed expeditions, stocked ports, and organized the first long-distance commercial networks.
duties: Source inventory, negotiate freight, manage credit to buyers, insure cargo, and balance books.
skills: Negotiation, logistics, accounting, market intelligence, risk appetite.
tools: Bills of lading, letters of credit, sample cases, ledgers, now ERP systems.
regions: Mediterranean republics, Hanseatic League, Indian Ocean, global trade today.

events.label: Oceanic trade routes
events.description: Portuguese and later European routes reshape spice and textile merchant geographies.

educationPath: Apprenticeship in trade houses, business school, family firm training.
modernSectors: Import-export, distribution, commodity houses, e-commerce sourcing.

name: Wholesaler
summary: A middle-tier distributor who aggregates goods from producers and sells in bulk to retailers—critical as factories outpaced local artisan supply in industrial cities.
duties: Warehouse inventory, price lists, delivery routes, credit terms, and returns handling.
skills: Inventory turns, route planning, B2B sales, category management.
tools: Warehouses, forklifts, barcodes, EDI ordering, cold-chain logistics.
regions: Industrial Europe and North America; global FMCG supply chains.

events.label: Containerized logistics
events.description: Standard containers lower freight cost and enable global wholesale networks.

educationPath: Business or supply-chain programs, distributor trainee roles.
modernSectors: Food service, hardware, pharmaceuticals, cash-and-carry clubs.

name: Importer
summary: A specialist in bringing foreign goods through customs, tariffs, and compliance—a role sharpened as colonial trade companies and later container ports globalized supply.
duties: Classify HS codes, arrange freight, pay duties, manage brokers, and clear regulatory holds.
skills: Customs law, Incoterms, supplier vetting, currency hedging, documentation.
tools: Customs entries, bonds, freight forwarder portals, compliance databases.
regions: Port cities worldwide—Rotterdam, Los Angeles, Singapore, Lagos.

events.label: GATT tariff rounds
events.description: Multilateral trade talks reshape import duty structures for postwar growth.

educationPath: International business, customs broker license, trade compliance certs.
modernSectors: Consumer goods, industrial inputs, food, automotive parts.

name: Exporter
summary: A trader who ships domestic products abroad, navigating quotas, phytosanitary rules, and payment risk—paired with importers to complete the circular flow of world commerce.
duties: Find overseas buyers, prepare export docs, arrange logistics, and secure payment instruments.
skills: Market entry strategy, documentation, cultural fluency, credit insurance knowledge.
tools: Commercial invoices, certificates of origin, letters of credit, freight quotes.
regions: Manufacturing hubs exporting to global demand centers.

events.label: Export credit agencies
events.description: Government-backed insurers help firms sell capital goods into emerging markets.

educationPath: Trade finance courses, export management programs, language skills.
modernSectors: Agriculture, machinery, software services, textiles, energy equipment.

name: Factor
summary: A commission agent who sold goods on consignment for producers—especially in cotton and dry goods—before integrated retailers absorbed their credit and distribution functions.
duties: Receive shipments, market samples, extend buyer credit, remit proceeds minus commission.
skills: Sample selling, credit judgment, correspondence, warehouse coordination.
tools: Showrooms, swatch books, ledgers, rail receipts.
regions: Antebellum U.S. South, British textile districts, European commission houses.
originLabel: Commission houses in plantation and factory trade

events.label: Vertical retail integration
events.description: Department stores and branded manufacturers reduce reliance on independent factors.

name: Underwriter
summary: A risk evaluator who decides whether to accept insurance or securities offerings and at what price—named for Lloyd's members writing their names under policy shares.
duties: Analyze applications, set terms and exclusions, price risk, and monitor portfolio concentrations.
skills: Risk selection, data interpretation, negotiation, regulatory compliance.
tools: Underwriting manuals, catastrophe models, credit scores, submission platforms.
regions: Insurance carriers, investment banks, mortgage lenders globally.

events.label: Catastrophe modeling
events.description: Hurricane Andrew accelerates computational disaster risk pricing in property lines.

educationPath: Insurance designations (AU, CPCU), bank credit training.
modernSectors: P&C, life, health, mortgage, securities IPO desks.

name: Credit Analyst
summary: A lender-side researcher who grades borrower financial health and recommends limits—institutionalized as bond rating agencies and bank credit departments matured.
duties: Spread financials, build projections, write memos, monitor covenants, and flag downgrades.
skills: Accounting analysis, industry research, writing, skepticism, spreadsheet modeling.
tools: Rating methodologies, 10-K filings, credit models, Bloomberg.
regions: Commercial banks, rating agencies, corporate treasury teams.
originLabel: Moody's manual and bank credit departments

events.label: Regulated bank ratings use
events.description: U.S. rules embed external ratings in capital requirements for bond holdings.

educationPath: Finance degree, CFA or CRC, bank credit academy.
modernSectors: Commercial lending, municipal finance, corporate bonds, fintech underwriting.

name: Financial Advisor
summary: A planner who helps households and executives invest, insure, and retire—professionalized as defined-contribution pensions shifted investment decisions to individuals.
duties: Assess goals, propose allocations, implement accounts, rebalance, and document suitability.
skills: Holistic planning, empathy, compliance, tax-aware investing, estate basics.
tools: Financial planning software, CRM, portfolio dashboards, risk questionnaires.
regions: North America, UK, Australia wealth management markets.
originLabel: Retail mutual fund and IRA advisory channels

events.label: Fiduciary rule debates
events.description: U.S. policy fights sharpen distinction between advice and product sales.

educationPath: CFP certification, Series 65, firm supervision programs.
modernSectors: RIAs, wirehouses, insurance-based planning, family offices.

name: Hedge Fund Manager
summary: A portfolio principal who pursues absolute returns with flexible strategies—popularized in the 1950s–60s as wealthy investors sought market-neutral and leveraged approaches.
duties: Raise capital, design strategy, hire analysts, risk-manage books, and report to LPs.
skills: Alpha generation, risk systems, investor relations, legal structuring.
tools: Prime brokerage, risk dashboards, alt-data feeds, legal side letters.
regions: Greenwich, New York, London, Singapore fund clusters.

events.label: LTCM crisis
events.description: Leverage and liquidity lessons reshape prime-broker oversight of hedge funds.

educationPath: Top finance pedigree, trading floor experience, legal and ops team.
modernSectors: Long-short equity, macro, quant, multi-strategy, crypto funds.

name: Private Equity Analyst
summary: A deal professional who evaluates leveraged buyouts and portfolio company improvements—boomed as pension funds sought private market returns beyond public equities.
duties: Screen targets, build LBO models, support diligence, monitor KPIs, and draft IC memos.
skills: Modeling, operational due diligence, PowerPoint, interview synthesis.
tools: Excel LBO templates, data rooms, management presentations.
regions: New York, London, Hong Kong buyout shops.

events.label: Record buyout boom
events.description: Cheap debt fuels mega-deals before global financial crisis repricing.

educationPath: Investment banking analyst program, MBA, PE associate track.
modernSectors: Buyouts, growth equity, infrastructure, secondaries funds.

name: Tax Advisor
summary: A specialist who structures transactions and filings to meet statutory tax obligations efficiently—grew complex as income taxes and corporate codes spread in the 20th century.
duties: Prepare returns, plan entity structures, research code, represent clients in disputes.
skills: Tax law, accounting, research, discretion, cross-border treaty knowledge.
tools: Tax prep software, research databases, engagement letters.
regions: Every jurisdiction with formal revenue authority.

events.label: Major reform cycles
events.description: Periodic tax overhauls create advisory surges in corporate and personal planning.

educationPath: Accounting or law degree, CPA or EA, LL.M. in tax optional.
modernSectors: Public accounting, law firms, in-house corporate tax, family offices.

name: Collections Agent
summary: A recovery specialist who pursues overdue debts by phone, letter, and legal escalation—institutionalized as consumer credit cards and medical billing expanded delinquency workflows.
duties: Contact debtors, negotiate payment plans, document calls, and refer legal accounts.
skills: Negotiation under stress, regulation (FDCPA), empathy, persistence, recordkeeping.
tools: Dialers, collection software, skip-trace databases, payment portals.
regions: U.S., UK, emerging markets with consumer credit growth.
originLabel: Retail credit and third-party collection agencies

events.label: Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
events.description: U.S. law sets behavioral rules that shape collector scripts and compliance departments.

name: Cashier
summary: A point-of-sale worker who rings purchases, handles cash, and balances tills—ubiquitous once fixed-price retail replaced haggling in department stores and supermarkets.
duties: Scan items, process payments, issue receipts, reconcile drawers, assist returns.
skills: Accuracy, speed, customer service, basic arithmetic, loss prevention awareness.
tools: Registers, barcode scanners, card terminals, self-checkout kiosks.
regions: Global retail, food service, entertainment venues.

events.label: Barcode POS adoption
events.description: Scanner checkout becomes default in supermarkets worldwide.

name: Shopkeeper
summary: The owner-operator of a retail storefront who selects stock, serves customers, and keeps neighborhood commerce alive—from colonial general stores to modern specialty boutiques.
duties: Order inventory, set prices, open and close shop, hire help, and manage local relationships.
skills: Merchandising, bookkeeping, community trust, supplier negotiation.
tools: Shelves, scales, ledgers, POS tablets, signage.
regions: Village markets, urban high streets, emerging-economy micro-retail.

events.label: Supermarket concentration
events.description: Chain grocery scale pressures independent grocers while niches survive.

educationPath: Family trade apprenticeship, small-business courses.
modernSectors: Convenience, specialty food, hardware, neighborhood services.

name: Auctioneer
summary: A fast-talking sale caller who converts competitive bids into binding contracts—used for livestock, art, foreclosures, and increasingly online timed auctions.
duties: Catalog lots, conduct bidding, authenticate hammer prices, and settle with buyers.
skills: Chant rhythm, crowd reading, legal disclaimers, valuation instincts.
tools: Gavel, bid paddles, online auction platforms, catalogues.
regions: British country sales, American farm auctions, global art houses.

events.label: eBay marketplace
events.description: Digital auctions democratize peer-to-peer bidding beyond live rooms.

educationPath: Auctioneer license schools, specialty appraisal training.
modernSectors: Art, livestock, industrial equipment, charity, foreclosure sales.

name: Appraiser
summary: A certified valuer who estimates fair market worth of property, gems, or collectibles—critical wherever lenders, insurers, and courts require independent worth opinions.
duties: Inspect assets, research comparables, write defensible reports, and testify if needed.
skills: Valuation methods, photography, report writing, ethics, specialty knowledge.
tools: Measuring devices, comp databases, USPAP standards, gem testers.
regions: Real estate boards, auction houses, insurance claims globally.

events.label: USPAP standardization
events.description: Uniform Standards professionalize appraisal practice in U.S. mortgage markets.

educationPath: Licensed appraiser coursework, designations (MAI, ASA).
modernSectors: Residential and commercial real estate, personal property, litigation support.

name: Bond Trader
summary: A fixed-income dealer who prices government and corporate debt—central to war finance and infrastructure funding since tradable bonds replaced informal court loans.
duties: Quote bid-ask spreads, manage duration risk, execute client orders, and watch central-bank policy.
skills: Macroeconomics, yield curve math, relative value, fast execution.
tools: Electronic trading platforms, Bloomberg, risk limits, repo desks.
regions: New York, London, Tokyo government and credit desks.
originLabel: Venetian and Dutch government bond markets

events.label: Floating exchange rates
events.description: Currency volatility increases hedging demand and sovereign bond trading volumes.

educationPath: Finance degree, sales and trading rotations, Series licenses.
modernSectors: Sell-side desks, asset managers, central bank open-market operations overlap.

name: Forex Trader
summary: A currency market participant who trades FX pairs for hedging or speculation—exploded after Bretton Woods fixed rates ended and electronic platforms democratized access.
duties: Monitor central banks, execute spot and forward trades, manage leverage, and report exposures.
skills: Macro news reaction, technical analysis, risk limits, multilingual news scanning.
tools: FX platforms, news wires, algo execution, position blotters.
regions: London, Singapore, New York interbank and retail FX.

events.label: Retail FX platforms
events.description: Online brokers open currency trading to non-institutional participants.

educationPath: Economics, bank markets training, risk management courses.
modernSectors: Banks, hedge funds, corporate treasury, retail brokerages.

name: Commodities Broker
summary: An intermediary who executes futures and options orders for hedgers and speculators—evolved from pit runners to FCM desks linked to global exchanges.
duties: Take client orders, explain margin, route fills, and ensure exchange compliance.
skills: Contract specs, margin math, client education, fast phone presence.
tools: Order routing systems, margin monitors, exchange rulebooks.
regions: Chicago, New York, London commodity brokerages.
originLabel: Grain pit brokerage after Civil War

events.label: Electronic pit decline
events.description: Open-outcry floors close as screen trading dominates commodity execution.

educationPath: Series 3 license, FCM training programs.
modernSectors: FCMs, introducing brokers, hedging advisory for producers.

name: Reinsurance Specialist
summary: A professional who insures insurance companies, spreading catastrophic losses across global balance sheets—matured in Swiss and Bermuda hubs after mega-disaster lessons.
duties: Structure treaties, price cat exposures, negotiate facultative covers, and model accumulations.
skills: Actuarial collaboration, contract drafting, relationship with cedents.
tools: Catastrophe models, treaty wordings, portfolio monitoring systems.
regions: Zurich, Munich, London, Bermuda reinsurance markets.
originLabel: Swiss Re and Cologne Re foundations

events.label: September 11 losses
events.description: Terror and casualty reinsurance pricing and exclusions reshape global treaties.

educationPath: Actuarial or underwriting track, reinsurance academies.
modernSectors: Property cat, life reinsurance, ILS and cat bonds.

name: Bill Collector
summary: A door-to-door or office worker who demanded payment on installment accounts—common before auto-debit and credit bureaus centralized delinquency handling.
duties: Visit debtors, record promises to pay, accept partial payments, and report skips.
skills: Persistence, local knowledge, tact, handwritten ledger accuracy.
tools: Collection books, receipt pads, street directories.
regions: Urban industrial districts with hire-purchase shops.

events.label: Phone collection agencies
events.description: Centralized dial operations begin replacing per-route walkers in many markets.

name: Comptroller
summary: A senior financial officer who oversees accounting, reporting, and internal controls—title used in corporations and governments alike for the chief fiscal watchdog role.
duties: Certify financial statements, manage controller staff, liaise with auditors, enforce policies.
skills: GAAP/IFRS, leadership, systems oversight, regulatory filings.
tools: ERP general ledgers, consolidation software, SOX control matrices.
regions: U.S. public companies, municipal finance offices.

events.label: SOX officer certifications
events.description: CEO and CFO sign-offs elevate comptroller and controller accountability.

educationPath: CPA, controller career ladder, MBA optional.
modernSectors: Fortune 500, universities, cities and states, nonprofits.

name: Treasurer
summary: An officer responsible for liquidity, banking relationships, and sometimes investor relations—distinct from accounting yet central to payroll, debt, and cash investment policy.
duties: Forecast cash, manage debt covenants, invest surpluses, oversee payments and FX hedges.
skills: Cash modeling, banking negotiation, debt markets, risk policy.
tools: Treasury workstations, payment hubs, debt schedules.
regions: Corporate HQs, nonprofits, municipal governments.

events.label: Money market innovation
events.description: New instruments and sweeps professionalize corporate cash management.

educationPath: Finance degree, CTP certification, banking rotations.
modernSectors: Corporate treasury, public finance, hospital systems.

name: Currency Dealer
summary: A wholesale trader in banknotes and coins for collectors, banks, and travelers—overlapping with numismatics and central-bank distribution channels.
duties: Source scarce denominations, authenticate notes, price inventories, and ship insured parcels.
skills: Grading standards, anti-counterfeit knowledge, market pricing, logistics.
tools: Loupe, UV lights, price guides, banknote sleeves.
regions: Major financial centers and airport duty-free networks.

events.label: Euro note launch
events.description: Currency changeovers create dealer demand for legacy note inventory.

name: Merchant Banker
summary: A private partnership banker who combined trade finance, acceptance bills, and venture stakes—prototype of modern investment banking before retail deposit giants absorbed many houses.
duties: Discount trade bills, advise family firms, place private securities, and syndicate risky ventures.
skills: Relationship banking, trade law, discretion, international correspondence.
tools: Acceptance stamps, partner ledgers, telex, club deals.
regions: City of London, Hamburg, Hong Kong historic houses.

events.label: Big Bang deregulation
events.description: London reforms accelerate consolidation into universal banking groups.

name: Loan Officer
summary: A frontline lender who interviews borrowers and packages credit applications for approval—standard role in banks and credit unions as consumer and commercial lending scaled with branch networks.
duties: Take applications, verify income, explain products, submit to underwriting, and close loans.
skills: Interviewing, credit guideline knowledge, sales ethics, documentation accuracy.
tools: Loan origination systems, credit reports, disclosure forms, e-sign platforms.
regions: U.S. banks, credit unions, mortgage shops, development finance institutions.

events.label: Mortgage crisis reforms
events.description: Ability-to-repay rules and QM standards reshape loan officer compensation and compliance.

educationPath: NMLS licensing for mortgages, bank training programs, SAFE Act compliance.
modernSectors: Retail banking, mortgage, SBA lending, auto finance.
